summary: Parallel review: FAIL (9 critical, 8 warning, 5 suggestion). Compound gate: WARN

findingsList.text: 🔴 iteration-escalation — Persistent eval warning: fabricated-refs recurred in iterations 1, 2

`run.mjs:1278` builds `allText = roleFindings.map(f => f.output).join("\n")` from raw role outputs (simplicity is in `PARALLEL_REVIEW_ROLES`), `parseFindings` tags any 🔴 as critical regardless of source role, `computeVerdict` returns FAIL when `critical > 0`, and `run.mjs:1316` sets `reviewFailed = true`. The `[simplicity veto]` label in `mergeReviewFindings` is display-only and intentionally NOT in the verdict path — clean layering. Test at `flows.test.mjs:276-289` mirrors the production path exactly.

🟡 test/flows.test.mjs:282 — Comment cites `run.mjs:1221-1222` but the actual multi-review verdict path is at `run.mjs:1278-1279`; update the cross-reference so future readers land on the correct lines.

The stated task ("simplicity 🔴 in multi-review → FAIL") is correctly implemented: simplicity is in `PARALLEL_REVIEW_ROLES`, all role outputs are joined and run through `parseFindings`/`computeVerdict`, and any 🔴 yields FAIL. Direct test at `test/flows.test.mjs:276-289` confirms the exact scenario. All 590 tests pass.
- `bin/lib/run.mjs:1307-1308, 1342-1344` — multi-review joins all role outputs, parses, calls `computeVerdict`; any 🔴 ⇒ FAIL
- `test/flows.test.mjs:276-289` — exact spec test (architect 🔵 + engineer no-findings + simplicity 🔴 → FAIL) passes

🟡 test/flows.test.mjs:285-287 — Test re-implements production verdict pipeline (`join` → `parseFindings` → `computeVerdict`) instead of asserting against `run.mjs`'s real code path; refactor into a small extracted helper or spy-based integration test so the test stays coupled to production.

🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:1268-1296 — Build-verify `simplicity-review` block lacks a dedicated test exercising SKIP console branch, `reviewRounds` increment, and `lastFailure` composition; add an integration-style test mirroring the parallel-review style.
🔵 test/flows.test.mjs:276-289 — Add inverse case: simplicity 🟡 + others 🔵 → assert `verdict === "PASS"` and `backlog === true`.

Tests: 590/590 pass. Stated task ("simplicity 🔴 in multi-review → FAIL") is verified by `flows.test.mjs:276-289` and the production path at `run.mjs:1299-1344`. Eval written to `tasks/task-2/eval-tester.md`.
- Safe defaults: empty output → `SKIP` (logged warning), not silent PASS; `!reviewFailed` guard preserved; only 🔴 critical sets `reviewFailed`.
🟡 bin/agt.mjs:340 — `etime=` column requested in `ps -o state=,etime=` is never parsed; remove `etime=` or implement the staleness check the comment alludes to
🟡 bin/lib/flows.mjs:210 — `evaluateSimplicityOutput` has a single call site at `run.mjs:1273`; inline the SKIP guard until a second consumer materializes
🟡 bin/agt.mjs:324-376 — `isAgtRunning` refactor (drop per-project scoping) is unrelated to the simplicity-reviewer task; should land separately

Stated task verified: `simplicity` ∈ `PARALLEL_REVIEW_ROLES` (flows.mjs:170); multi-review joins raw outputs and runs `parseFindings`+`computeVerdict` (run.mjs:1307-1315), making the path role-agnostic. Test at `flows.test.mjs:276-289` asserts the exact "simplicity 🔴 + others 🔵/none → FAIL" scenario (590/590 tests pass). Eval written to `tasks/task-2/eval-simplicity.md`.
